<!-- PROSE — edit freely in Obsidian -->

A verse fairy tale written in the style of a Russian bylina — the traditional oral epic of heroes and bogatyrs. The poem follows Volga, a heroic warrior of the Volga river, and draws on the imagery and rhythms of Slavic folk poetry. Tolstoy wrote it alongside its companion piece *Oaf* in 1871, the same period as his work on the *ABC Book*, when he was deeply engaged with Russian folk tradition as a source of moral and aesthetic truth.
